cubes.title: SKV Batch Cube Upload Implementation
rules: Create a POST /api/v1/cubes/batch endpoint accepting an array of cube objects
Validate and index each cube individually using existing single-upload logic
Ensure newly uploaded cubes are immediately searchable post-upload
Use a transactional approach to handle errors and prevent partial failures
cubes.rationale: Enables efficient, reliable, and protocol-compliant ingestion of multiple knowledge units while maintaining system integrity and searchability.

cubes.title: SKV Cube Priority & Conflict Resolution
rules: Apply hierarchical priority: 1 (Constitutional) > 2 (Basic) > 3 (Experience) > 4 (Project)
Resolve ties by higher semantic version, then later update date
If priority, version, and date are identical, apply both rules and notify user of contradiction
Users cannot override Priority 1 cubes; may override Priority 3/4 per session with AI warning and logging
When users disagree, explain the rule's rationale and offer a compliant alternative
Always state which cube was applied and the reason in every conflict
cubes.rationale: Ensures deterministic rule application, maintains system integrity, and provides transparent conflict handling within the SKV framework.

cubes.title: Clean Project Root Directory Organization
rules: Group related files by concern (source, tests, config, docs)
Keep the root directory flat and avoid deep nesting
Follow language/framework conventions for directory naming (e.g., src/ for Python)
Exclude generated files, lock files, and IDE artifacts using .gitignore
Include standard top-level directories: src/, tests/, docs/, scripts/, config/, data/, assets/, .github/
Include standard root files: .gitignore, README.md, LICENSE, CONTRIBUTING.md, CHANGELOG.md, and a task runner (Makefile/Justfile)
cubes.rationale: Ensures maintainability, improves team collaboration, and streamlines developer onboarding.

cubes.title: API Error Handling Best Practices
rules: Define a standard JSON structure for all error responses (e.g., RFC 7807)
Map errors to appropriate HTTP status codes (400, 401, 403, 404, 409, 422, 429, 500, 503)
Avoid returning HTTP 200 with an error flag in the response body
Provide specific, actionable error messages instead of generic ones
cubes.rationale: Ensures predictable client-side parsing, correct HTTP semantics, and improves developer debugging experience.

cubes.title: FastAPI JWT Authentication Implementation
rules: Install python-jose[cryptography], passlib[bcrypt], and python-multipart for JWT and auth handling
Use passlib with bcrypt for secure password hashing and verification
Use python-jose with HS256 algorithm and a secure SECRET_KEY for JWT creation/validation
Implement OAuth2PasswordBearer to extract tokens from incoming requests
Set appropriate ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES (e.g., 30) for security
cubes.rationale: Ensures secure, standardized authentication flow in FastAPI applications using industry-standard libraries.

cubes.title: FastAPI OAuth2 & JWT Implementation
rules: Install required packages: fastapi, uvicorn, python-jose[cryptography], passlib[bcrypt], python-multipart
Configure JWT constants: SECRET_KEY, ALGORITHM (HS256), and ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES
Initialize CryptContext with bcrypt for secure password hashing
Set up OAuth2PasswordBearer with the correct tokenUrl endpoint
Define Pydantic models for Token, TokenData, User, and UserInDB to handle request/response validation
Replace mock database dictionaries with a production-ready database in deployment
cubes.rationale: Establishes a secure, standardized authentication mechanism using industry-standard OAuth2 and JWT practices in FastAPI.

cubes.title: PostgreSQL Query Optimization
rules: Always start optimization with EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS, TIMING) to inspect the query planner
Add indexes to eliminate Seq Scans on large tables
Run ANALYZE to update statistics when row estimates significantly differ from actuals
Select index types based on query patterns: B-tree for equality/range, Partial for conditional subsets, Composite for multi-column (leftmost prefix rule), GIN for full-text
cubes.rationale: Ensures efficient query execution, reduces latency, and prevents unnecessary resource consumption.

cubes.title: Kubernetes Cluster Setup Prerequisites & kubeadm Init
rules: Disable swap on all Linux nodes before installing kubelet
Ensure unique hostname, MAC address, and product_uuid per node
Open required ports (6443, 2379-2380, 10250, etc.) for node communication
Install container runtime (containerd recommended) before kubeadm
Run sudo kubeadm init --pod-network-cidr <CIDR> to initialize the control plane
cubes.rationale: Ensures a stable, compliant Kubernetes cluster deployment by meeting core system and network requirements.

cubes.title: React WebSocket Implementation
rules: Use a custom hook to encapsulate WebSocket connection lifecycle and state management
Store WebSocket instance and retry counters in useRef to prevent re-renders and stale closures
Implement automatic reconnection with configurable intervals and max retry limits
Always clean up WebSocket connections in useEffect cleanup functions to prevent memory leaks
Wrap message parsing in try/catch blocks to handle malformed JSON safely
cubes.rationale: Ensures stable, memory-safe, and production-ready real-time communication in React applications.

cubes.title: Redis Session Management Best Practices
rules: Use Redis for session storage to leverage in-memory speed and sub-millisecond reads/writes
Configure TTL/EXPIRE to automatically clean up idle sessions
Utilize atomic operations (HSET, INCR) for safe concurrent session updates
Enable RDB or AOF persistence for session data recovery
Deploy Redis Cluster or Sentinel for high availability and horizontal scaling
cubes.rationale: Ensures fast, secure, and scalable session handling with automatic cleanup and fault tolerance.
